original_hypothesis.title: Reward Token Transfer Failure Permanently Bricks All User Reward Claims
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: If any reward token becomes non-transferable (paused, blacklisted, or reverts on transfer), the _getRewards function permanently reverts for all affected users. Since reward tokens cannot be removed from the rewardTokens array, and _getRewards iterates all tokens in a single transaction, failure of one token blocks claiming of ALL reward tokens.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The _getRewards function loops through all rewardTokens and performs safeTransfer for each expired lock item. If any single token reverts, the entire transaction fails. There's no mechanism to skip individual tokens or remove problematic ones from the array.
deployment_assumptions: One or more reward tokens could become non-transferable (pause, blacklist, or upgrade)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: LockingMultiRewards.s.sol adds ARB as reward token. If ARB or any future reward token implements pause/blacklist functionality, this risk materializes.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _getRewards iterates all rewardTokens in single tx. safeTransfer at line 483 reverts if token is non-transferable. No try-catch, no skip mechanism, no removeReward function.

preconditions: At least one reward token becomes non-transferable (paused, blacklisted, upgraded)
Users have non-zero accumulated reward amounts for the failed token
The reward lock for the user has expired (triggering safeTransfer)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: rewardTokens array is append-only (no removal). _getRewards loops all tokens atomically.
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: External token governance action (pause/blacklist) creates cascading failure
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct attacker profit, but all user rewards across ALL tokens become permanently frozen

economic_questions: Do any configured reward tokens have pause/blacklist capability?
What is the total accumulated reward value at risk?
Can users still withdraw staking tokens without claiming rewards?
validation_requirements: Verify withdraw() doesn't call _getRewards (it doesn't - separate path)
Test _getRewards behavior when one reward token reverts on transfer

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: N/A - depends on external token governance
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: N/A for attacker; all accumulated rewards at risk

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete reward claiming failure across all tokens

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All stakers with accumulated rewards for the problematic token
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% of all accumulated rewards frozen (not just the failed token)
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All pending and locked rewards across all reward tokens

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Trace _getRewards when safeTransfer reverts at any index
investigation_priorities.execution_complexity: depends on external token - outside attacker control

validation_result.mechanism: Atomic reward loop with no skip/remove mechanism
validation_result.impact_summary: If any reward token reverts on transfer (paused, blacklisted, upgraded), all users with non-zero locked reward amounts for that token permanently lose the ability to claim ALL rewards across every reward token.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The `_getRewards` function in LockingMultiRewards.sol iterates through all entries in the `rewardTokens` array in a single transaction. For expired reward locks, it calls `rewardToken.safeTransfer(user, amount)` for each token. If any single token reverts (e.g., due to being paused or blacklisted), the entire transaction reverts, blocking claims for ALL reward tokens—not just the problematic one. The `rewardTokens` array is append-only via `addReward`, and there is no `removeReward` function, no try-catch around transfers, and no mechanism to skip individual tokens. This means once a reward token becomes non-transferable, the situation is permanent and unrecoverable through existing contract functions. Users CAN still call `withdraw()` to retrieve staked tokens, but `getRewards()` and `withdrawWithRewards()` are permanently bricked for any user who has accumulated a non-zero locked reward amount for the problematic token.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The defect is clearly present in the code. Lines 474-502 of LockingMultiRewards.sol show the atomic loop over all reward tokens with no error handling. The `addReward` function (lines 300-312) only appends to the array. No `removeReward` function exists anywhere in the contract. The `safeTransfer` at approximately line 483 will revert the entire transaction if the token is non-transferable.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The loop iterates ALL rewardTokens atomically. A single safeTransfer revert on any token blocks the entire function. There is no try-catch, no skip mechanism, and no ability to remove reward tokens from the array.
The rewardTokens array is append-only. There is no removeReward function anywhere in the contract, making a problematic token permanently part of the iteration in _getRewards.

calling_sequence: 2. _updateRewardsForUser(msg.sender) updates reward accounting for all tokens
5. For each token with item.amount > 0, calls rewardToken.safeTransfer(user, amount)
6. If ANY safeTransfer reverts, entire transaction reverts
7. User cannot claim rewards for ANY token
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: getRewards() -> _getRewards(user) -> loop over rewardTokens -> rewardToken.safeTransfer(user, amount) at line ~483. If the rewardToken at any index i has become non-transferable AND the user has a non-zero item.amount for that index from a previous epoch, the safeTransfer reverts. Because there is no try-catch and no way to skip or remove the token, the function permanently reverts for affected users.

deployment_assumptions_checked: One or more reward tokens could become non-transferable (pause, blacklist, or upgrade)
config_validation.deployment_evidence: LockingMultiRewards.s.sol adds ARB as reward token: staking.addReward(toolkit.getAddress(block.chainid, "arb")). ARB on Arbitrum is a standard ERC20 without native pause/blacklist. However, the code defect exists regardless of which token is currently configured—any future token added via addReward could trigger this. The owner has no recourse once a problematic token is added.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Scenario: (1) Owner adds reward token X via addReward. (2) Operator distributes rewards for token X via notifyRewardAmount. (3) Users stake and accumulate reward lock items with non-zero amounts for token X. (4) Token X's governance pauses transfers or blacklists the LockingMultiRewards contract. (5) When users' reward locks expire and they call getRewards(), the safeTransfer for token X reverts, blocking claims for ALL reward tokens. (6) There is no removeReward function. (7) Users can still withdraw() staked tokens but permanently lose access to all accumulated rewards. This is realistic for tokens with governance-controlled pause mechanisms.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. All users with non-zero locked reward amounts for the failing token lose access to ALL their accumulated rewards across every reward token. Staked tokens can still be withdrawn via withdraw(), but all reward value is permanently frozen. The impact scales with the total accumulated rewards in the contract and affects every staker who interacted with the system while the problematic token was distributing rewards.
recommended_mitigations: Add a removeReward(address token) function callable by onlyOwner that removes a token from the rewardTokens array and handles cleanup of existing reward lock items
Wrap the safeTransfer call in a try-catch block so that failure of one token does not block claims for other tokens
Add a per-token claim function (e.g., getReward(address token)) that allows users to claim individual reward tokens independently
Add an emergency function that allows the owner to mark a reward token as skippable, causing _getRewards to bypass its transfer
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy LockingMultiRewards with a normal staking token. 2. Add two reward tokens: a normal ERC20 and a pausable ERC20 (e.g., MockPausableToken). 3. Have a user stake and lock tokens. 4. Distribute rewards for both tokens via notifyRewardAmount. 5. Advance time past one epoch so rewards accumulate and lock items are created. 6. Call getRewards() once to create lock items with amounts. 7. Advance time past another epoch. 8. Pause the pausable token. 9. Call getRewards() again — verify it reverts. 10. Verify the user cannot claim any rewards from either token. 11. Verify withdraw() still works.
uncertainties: Whether currently configured reward tokens (ARB) have pause/blacklist capability — ARB does not natively, but tokens can be upgraded
Whether future reward tokens added by the owner might have such capabilities
Whether the owner would add a token with such properties — this is an operational risk that the code should defend against
additional_testing_needed: Test with a mock pausable ERC20 to confirm the exact revert behavior
Test that withdraw() remains functional when getRewards() is bricked
Test the scenario where a user has zero accumulated rewards for the problematic token — verify they can still claim other rewards
Test gas costs of _getRewards with maximum reward tokens (5) to understand bounded gas costs
